title: Accommodation amenities & features — complete write path, i18n catalog, admin chips UI

tasks.title: Add amenityIds and featureIds to accommodation CRUD schemas
tasks.description:
Add optional `amenityIds: z.string().uuid().array().optional()` and `featureIds: z.string().uuid().array().optional()` to both `AccommodationCreateInputSchema` and `AccommodationUpdateInputSchema`.

Files:
- `packages/schemas/src/entities/accommodation/accommodation.crud.schema.ts` (lines 25-73)

Semantics contract (document in JSDoc):
- Absent field (`undefined`) = 'do not touch junctions' (on update only; on create treated as empty)
- `[]` empty array = 'remove all junctions'
- `[id1, id2]` = 'set exactly this set'

Test requirements:
- Test file: `packages/schemas/test/entities/accommodation/accommodation.crud.schema.test.ts`
- Assert: create schema accepts valid UUID arrays, accepts absent fields, accepts empty arrays
- Assert: rejects non-UUID strings in the arrays
- Assert: update schema same behavior
- Assert: absent amenityIds/featureIds does not cause schema parse failure

AC: AC-1.1, AC-1.2, AC-1.3

tasks.title: Implement transactional amenity junction sync in AccommodationService
tasks.description:
Add private helper `syncAmenityJunction(tx, accommodationId, amenityIds)` to `AccommodationService` and call it from `create` and `update` methods.

Files:
- `packages/service-core/src/services/accommodation/accommodation.service.ts`

Sync logic:
- On CREATE: if `amenityIds` provided (including empty), insert rows into `r_accommodation_amenity` for each ID within the same DB transaction. Validate each ID against the `amenity` catalog — any unknown ID rolls back the entire transaction with a typed `VALIDATION_ERROR` (not a generic DB error).
- On UPDATE: apply delete-absent / insert-added diff:
  1. Fetch current `amenityId` set for this accommodation
  2. Compute `toDelete = current − submitted`, `toInsert = submitted − current`
  3. DELETE rows where amenityId IN toDelete
  4. INSERT rows for toInsert (validate IDs against catalog before inserting)
  5. Existing rows (intersection) are untouched
  6. Unknown IDs in toInsert roll back the transaction
- If `amenityIds` is `undefined` on update, skip sync entirely (do not touch junctions)

DB schemas to import:
- `packages/db/src/schemas/accommodation/r_accommodation_amenity.dbschema.ts`
- `packages/db/src/schemas/accommodation/amenity.dbschema.ts` (for catalog validation)

Test requirements: covered by T-008 (pure service unit tests with real DB or mock). Write test stubs here if not using T-008 separately.

AC: AC-1.4, AC-1.5

subtasks.title: Wire into service.update with undefined guard

tasks.title: Implement transactional feature junction sync in AccommodationService
tasks.description:
Add private helper `syncFeatureJunction(tx, accommodationId, featureIds)` to `AccommodationService` and call it from `create` and `update` methods. Mirrors AC-1.4/AC-1.5 but targets `r_accommodation_feature`.

Files:
- `packages/service-core/src/services/accommodation/accommodation.service.ts`

Sync logic identical to T-002 but for features:
- On CREATE: insert into `r_accommodation_feature` within the same DB transaction; unknown featureId rolls back
- On UPDATE: delete-absent / insert-added diff against `r_accommodation_feature`; unknown IDs roll back
- `featureIds` absent on update = skip feature sync

DB schemas:
- `packages/db/src/schemas/accommodation/r_accommodation_feature.dbschema.ts`
- `packages/db/src/schemas/accommodation/feature.dbschema.ts` (catalog validation)

Can be implemented in the same file edit as T-002 (both helpers in one pass) or sequentially. Keep separate task ID to track AC coverage independently.

Test requirements: covered by T-008.

AC: AC-1.6

tasks.title: Write service-level unit tests for junction sync (PR1 quality gate)
tasks.description:
Write the unit/integration tests that constitute the PR1 quality gate for junction sync behavior. These tests exercise the service directly with a test database (following repo Test-Informed Development: pure service logic → test alongside or first).

Test file:
- `packages/service-core/test/services/accommodation/accommodation-junction-sync.test.ts` (NEW)

Test cases required per AC-1.8:
1. CREATE with valid `amenityIds` — assert junction rows exist in `r_accommodation_amenity` after create
2. CREATE with valid `featureIds` — assert junction rows exist in `r_accommodation_feature` after create
3. CREATE with unknown `amenityId` — assert entire transaction rolled back (accommodation row does not exist), error type is VALIDATION_ERROR
4. UPDATE that adds one amenity + removes another — assert diff applied: removed ID gone, added ID present, untouched ID unchanged
5. UPDATE with unknown `featureId` — assert rollback, typed error
6. UPDATE with absent `amenityIds` — assert existing junctions unchanged
7. UPDATE with `amenityIds: []` — assert all amenity junctions removed

Follows AAA pattern. Uses real test DB (pattern from SPEC-080 service integration tests) OR mocks the Drizzle transaction if project test infra uses mocking approach.

AC: AC-1.8

tasks.title: Migrate amenity catalog DB schema: name/description → nameI18n/descriptionI18n JSONB
tasks.description:
Migrate the `amenity` table's plain-text `name` and `description` columns to JSONB i18n columns.

Files:
- `packages/db/src/schemas/accommodation/amenity.dbschema.ts`
  - Replace `name: text('name').notNull()` with `nameI18n: jsonb('name_i18n').notNull()`
  - Replace `description: text('description')` with `descriptionI18n: jsonb('description_i18n')`
  - JSONB TypeScript type annotation: `{ es: string; en: string; pt: string }`

Migration strategy:
- Run `pnpm db:generate` to produce a Drizzle migration file under `packages/db/drizzle/`
- The generated migration must: add `name_i18n` JSONB column, copy existing `name` value into all three locales (`{es: name, en: name, pt: name}`), then drop old `name` column. Same for `description` → `description_i18n`.
- Review the generated SQL before committing — ensure the data-copy step is present
- After migration: run `packages/db/scripts/apply-postgres-extras.sh` (triggers/search_index are invisible to Drizzle — required per project CLAUDE.md)

Test requirements:
- Test file: `packages/db/test/schemas/amenity.dbschema.test.ts`
- Assert TypeScript shape: `nameI18n` is typed as `{ es: string; en: string; pt: string }` not `string`
- Assert: column exists in schema object (Drizzle schema introspection)

AC: AC-2.3, AC-2.9

tasks.title: Create MultiSelectChipsField admin component
tasks.description:
Create the `MultiSelectChipsField` component in the admin accommodation form's field family.

Files (NEW):
- `apps/admin/src/features/accommodations/components/MultiSelectChipsField.tsx`

Component contract:
- Props: `{ fetchUrl: string; value: string[]; onChange: (ids: string[]) => void; label: string; placeholder?: string }`
- Fetches catalog on mount via `GET fetchUrl` using TanStack Query with 5-minute `staleTime` (client-side cache)
- Renders a searchable multi-select chip list
- Display name: use `nameI18n.es` from the catalog item, fallback to `nameI18n.en` if `es` is absent (AC-3.2)
- Loading state: render a skeleton (use existing Shadcn `Skeleton` component)
- Error state: inline error message, no crash (AC-3.7)
- Empty catalog: render empty-state message, not a blank area (AC-3.8)
- Chip toggling: clicking a selected chip deselects it; clicking an unselected chip selects it
- Search: filter visible chips by `nameI18n.es` (case-insensitive substring match)

Style: Tailwind CSS utility classes (admin app convention — NOT CSS modules)

Test requirements (alongside implementation):
- Test file: `apps/admin/src/features/accommodations/components/MultiSelectChipsField.test.tsx`
- Assert: renders skeleton while fetch in flight (mock TanStack Query loading state)
- Assert: renders chips with `es` locale names after successful fetch
- Assert: renders error message on fetch failure, component does not crash
- Assert: renders empty-state message when catalog returns zero items
- Assert: clicking chip toggles selection and calls `onChange` with updated array
- Assert: search input filters visible chips

AC: AC-3.1, AC-3.2, AC-3.7, AC-3.8

tasks.title: Rewrite quality-score 'services' signal to use amenities.length
tasks.description:
Replace the `countTruthyKeys` call in the `'services'` signal with a direct check on `amenities.length` from the junction-backed array.

Files:
- `apps/admin/src/features/accommodations/config/score-signals.ts` (lines 198-225)

Current (to REMOVE): signal evaluates `countTruthyKeys(values, ['hasWifi','hasAirConditioning','hasParking','hasPool','hasKitchen','hasPetFriendly','hasGym','hasBreakfast'])`

Replacement logic:
```typescript
// Full credit: 3+ amenities selected
// Partial credit: 1-2 amenities selected  
// No credit: 0 amenities
const count = values.amenities?.length ?? 0;
if (count >= 3) return FULL_CREDIT;       // whatever full-credit constant/value the engine uses
if (count >= 1) return PARTIAL_CREDIT;    // partial credit constant/value
return 0;
```

Preserve the existing signal structure — only change the evaluation function body. The signal `id: 'services'` stays the same.

Do NOT modify:
- `apps/admin/src/components/quality-score/compute-score.ts`
- `apps/admin/src/components/quality-score/AccommodationQualityScore.tsx`

Test requirements:
- Test file: `apps/admin/src/features/accommodations/config/score-signals.test.ts`
- Assert: signal returns full credit for `amenities.length >= 3`
- Assert: signal returns partial credit for `amenities.length === 1` and `=== 2`
- Assert: signal returns 0 for `amenities.length === 0` (empty array)
- Assert: signal returns 0 for `amenities` undefined
- Assert: `compute-score.ts` and `AccommodationQualityScore.tsx` are NOT modified (diff check in test or comment)

AC: AC-3.5, AC-3.6

- The SQL definitions for the view and triggers (likely in `packages/db/scripts/` or `packages/db/src/triggers/`)

Changes required:
- In the trigger/view that builds `search_index` text content: replace any reference to the old plain-text `amenity.name` or `feature.name` columns with `amenity.name_i18n->>'es'` and `feature.name_i18n->>'es'`
- Remove any boolean column references in the trigger if any existed (per AC-4.2 — confirmed: spec says 'No boolean column reference remains')
- After editing: run the script to apply changes to the dev DB

R-3 risk mitigation: this task ships in PR4 which comes after PR2 (where the column rename happened). By PR4, the old `name` column no longer exists — the trigger MUST use `name_i18n`. Confirm the script applies cleanly.

Test requirements:
- Test file: `packages/db/test/triggers/search-index.test.ts` (existing or new)
- Assert: after inserting an accommodation with amenities (via junction), `search_index` entry includes the amenity `nameI18n.es` string in the searchable text
- Assert: `SELECT to_tsvector(...)` from `search_index` for a known accommodation includes 'wifi' (or equivalent amenity slug text)

AC: AC-4.2

tasks.title: Update AccommodationWithFeaturesSchema read paths in web layer

tasks.title: PR4 quality gate — full typecheck, lint, test run across all packages
tasks.description:
Run the full PR4 quality gate — the most comprehensive gate since it validates the entire spec end-to-end.

Checks:
1. `pnpm typecheck` across ALL packages and apps — must pass clean (AC-4.4)
2. `pnpm lint` (Biome) — zero errors in all changed files
3. `pnpm test` — all test suites green:
   - Web component tests (T-023, T-027, T-028)
   - Fixture tests (T-025)
   - Search index trigger test (T-024)
   - TypeScript compilation of fixture updates
4. Regression check: all previously passing tests from PR1/PR2/PR3 still pass
5. Manual smoke (optional but recommended): run `pnpm db:fresh-dev` + `pnpm db:seed` and verify:
   - Accommodation create/update with amenityIds via API
   - Admin form chips pre-populate and submit correctly
   - Web detail page shows junction-sourced amenity names

This is the final gate — do not open PR4 until this passes.
